title: The minute before a meeting starts
summary: Capture the quiet, strange minute before a class or meeting begins, when everyone waits and small things become loud.
topic: The short waiting time just before a meeting or class begins
prompt: Write 8–10 short sentences about the minute before a class, meeting, or event starts. Describe what people do, what small sounds you hear, and how time feels in that minute. Use present simple, past simple, and adverbs of frequency.
hintStarters: In that one minute I always hear…
sampleText: I like the minute before a meeting starts. It is a strange and quiet minute. People sit down and open their notebooks. Some people check their phones one last time. The boss is not in the room yet. Everyone waits. In that one minute I always hear small things. I hear a chair move. I hear someone cough near the window. Two people whisper at the back. The clock on the wall ticks slowly. Time feels strange because nothing happens yet, but something is about to happen. I always feel a little nervous in this minute. Yesterday the meeting started two minutes late. So we waited and the room was very still. A man tapped his pen on the table. A woman read her notes again. Then the door opened and the boss came in. Everyone sat up straight and the quiet minute ended. I think this little pause is the most honest part of the day. Nobody pretends. We just wait together for the day to begin.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the minute before a meeting starts
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: phút trước khi cuộc họp bắt đầu

sampleAnnotations.phrase: is not in the room yet

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi nghe một chiếc ghế xê dịch

sampleAnnotations.phrase: The clock on the wall ticks slowly
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Chiếc đồng hồ trên tường kêu tích tắc chậm rãi
có điều gì đó sắp xảy ra

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the meeting started two minutes late
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cuộc họp bắt đầu trễ hai phút
Chúng tôi chỉ cùng nhau chờ đợi

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for the usual minute, past simple for yesterday
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng hiện tại đơn để tả cái phút quen thuộc thường lặp lại ('Everyone waits', 'The clock ticks slowly') và quá khứ đơn để kể chuyện hôm qua ('the meeting started two minutes late', 'the door opened'). Hãy chuyển thì rõ ràng khi chuyển từ thói quen sang một lần cụ thể.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present simple for the usual minute ('Everyone waits', 'The clock ticks slowly') and the past simple for yesterday's events ('the meeting started two minutes late', 'the door opened'). Switch tenses clearly when you move from a habit to one occasion.

sampleTranslationVi: Tôi thích cái phút trước khi cuộc họp bắt đầu. Đó là một phút kỳ lạ và yên tĩnh. Mọi người ngồi xuống và mở sổ tay. Vài người kiểm tra điện thoại lần cuối. Sếp vẫn chưa ở trong phòng. Mọi người chờ. Trong cái phút đó tôi luôn nghe thấy những điều nhỏ. Tôi nghe một chiếc ghế xê dịch. Tôi nghe ai đó ho gần cửa sổ. Hai người thì thầm ở phía sau. Chiếc đồng hồ trên tường kêu tích tắc chậm rãi. Thời gian cảm giác kỳ lạ vì chưa có gì xảy ra, nhưng có điều gì đó sắp xảy ra. Tôi luôn thấy hơi hồi hộp trong cái phút này. Hôm qua cuộc họp bắt đầu trễ hai phút. Nên chúng tôi đợi và căn phòng rất tĩnh lặng. Một người đàn ông gõ bút lên bàn. Một người phụ nữ đọc lại ghi chú của mình. Rồi cánh cửa mở ra và sếp bước vào. Mọi người ngồi thẳng dậy và cái phút yên tĩnh kết thúc. Tôi nghĩ khoảng dừng nhỏ này là phần chân thật nhất của ngày. Không ai giả vờ. Chúng tôi chỉ cùng nhau chờ ngày bắt đầu.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple form: 'The clock ___ slowly.'
options: tick
ticks
ticking
ticked
mcQuestions.hint: The subject is 'the clock' — what ending does the verb need?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses an adverb of frequency correctly?
options: I feel always a little nervous in this minute.
Always I feel a little nervous nervous.
I always feel a little nervous in this minute.
I always feeling a little nervous in this minute.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'always' goes before the main verb: 'I always feel'.
mcQuestions.hint: Where does 'always' go with a main verb?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses the past simple for yesterday?
options: Yesterday the meeting starts two minutes late.
Yesterday the meeting is started two minutes late.
Yesterday the meeting starting two minutes late.
Yesterday the meeting started two minutes late.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'started' is the correct past simple of 'start' for 'yesterday'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best explains why time feels strange?
options: Time feels strange because nothing happens yet.
Time feels strange because nothing happen yet.
Time feels strange so nothing yet happens.
Because nothing yet time feels strange happens.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because' + full clause; 'nothing' takes a singular verb 'happens'.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'because' you need subject + verb, and 'nothing' is singular.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct verb: 'Everyone ___ for the meeting.'
options: wait
waits
waiting
are wait

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence sounds the most natural describing a sound?
options: I hear someone coughs near the window.
I hears someone cough near the window.
I hear someone cough near the window.
I hear someone cough near the window machine.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'hear someone cough' (object + bare infinitive) is correct, and 'I hear' takes no -s.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'hear someone', the next verb has no 'to' and no -s.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses the past simple for the door?
options: Then the door opened and the boss comed in.
Then the door open and the boss came in.
Then the door is opened and the boss came in.
Then the door opened and the boss came in.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'opened' (regular) and 'came' (irregular) are both correct past-simple forms.
mcQuestions.hint: Check both verbs: one regular, one irregular.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is the most natural and on-topic?
options: Some people check their phones one last time.
Some people checks their phones one last time.
Some people check their phone one last times.
Some people check their phones one last time to begin meeting.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'people check' (plural, no -s) and 'one last time' are both natural and correct.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes the waiting room most naturally?
options: So we waited and the room were very still.
So we waited and the room was very still.
So we wait and the room was very still.
So we waited and the room was very stilly.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'we waited' (past) and 'the room was' (singular past) are both correct.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the past verb and 'the room was' (singular).

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is the most natural reflection at the end?
options: We just waits together for the day to begin.
We just wait together for the day to begins.
We just wait together for the day to begin.
We just wait together for the day begin to.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'we wait' (no -s) and 'for the day to begin' (to + base verb) are both correct.
mcQuestions.hint: With 'we', no -s; after 'the day' use 'to begin'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do you think small sounds, like a cough or a ticking clock, feel so loud in the quiet minute before something begins?
